When a rectangle has side lengths in the ratio of $2:5$, extending all of its sides by $9 \text{ cm}$ changes the ratio to $3:7$. This means that the original sides are in the ratio $2:5$, and after extension, they are in the ratio $3:7$. 

*highlighted section* The original rectangle has sides of lengths $2x$ and $5x$, where $x$ is some positive real number. After extending all sides by $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths become $2x + 9$ and $5x + 9$. 

*highlighted section* The new ratio is given as $3:7$, so we can set up the equation:

$$
\frac{2x + 9}{5x + 9} = \frac{3}{7}
$$

*highlighted section* Solving this equation gives:

$$
7(2x + 9) = 3(5x + 9)
$$

$$
14x + 63 = 15x + 27
$$

$$
14x + 63 = 15x + 27
$$

$$
63 - 27 = 15x - 14x
$$

$$
36 = x
$$

*highlighted section* So, the original side lengths are $2x = 72$ and $5x = 180$. After extending all sides by $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths are $72 + 9 = 81$ and $180 + 9 = 189$.

*highlighted section* The new ratio is $81:189$, which simplifies to $3:7$ as given. 

*highlighted section* If we extend all sides by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$, the new side lengths will be $81 + 9 = 90$ and $189 + 9 = 198$. The new ratio is $90:198$, which simplifies to $5:11$.

*highlighted section* Therefore, the final ratio of the side lengths after extending all sides by an additional $9 \text{ cm}$ is $5:11$.
